forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure extends from a high south of WA, across waters well south of SA. The high slowly moves eastwards to be centred over the Tasman Sea by Saturday afternoon, with further high pressure systems building well south of the Bight. A trough over inland WA is expected to move across western and northern SA on Sunday and into the new week. A cold front clips the southeast early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Possible showers and the chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures above average. East to northeasterly winds, with local sea breezes.
Possible isolated showers and the chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Dry elsewhere. East to southeast winds, fresh about the north.

Showers, about the north and west. Chance of a thunderstorm in the far west. Maximum temperatures above average, tending near to below average in the north and southwest. Southeast to northeasterly winds, with local sea breezes.

Showers, extending statewide, tending to rain about the north with locally heavy falls possible. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperature below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Showers contracting to the Pastoral districts. Rain at times about the north with locally heavy falls possible. The chance of a thunderstorm in the north. Maximum temperature below average. Southeast to easterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Broadly less than 5mm, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the south of Pastoral districts, reaching 30 to 60 mm about the north and 50 to 100 mm about the far northwest, with possible isolated falls greater than 100 mm. Isolated falls of 5 to 20 mm are possible about parts of the agricultural area and the southwest.
